channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Experts warn Kalai II hydropower project in Arunachal Pradesh may threaten critically endangered white-bellied heron and fragile Himalayan ecosystems
item.description: The Union Environment Ministry's Forest Advisory Committee has granted in-principle approval to the 1,200 MW Kalai II hydropower project on the Lohit River in Arunachal Pradesh, despite concerns from ...

item.title: Zanzibar strengthens digital transformation strategy through IIT Madras partnership and investment in innovation
item.description: The Revolutionary Government of Zanzibar has reaffirmed its commitment to transforming the islands into a leading technology and innovation hub in Africa by investing in digital infrastructure, artifi...

item.title: Senator Mike Lee Accused of False Claims About White Supremacist March
item.description: A U.S. Senator faced accusations Sunday that he'd lost his grip on reality after he repeatedly claimed, without evidence, that Democrats were responsible for a white supremacist march in Washington D....

item.title: Trump Addresses Political Priorities at Weather-Delayed July 4th Celebrations
item.description: The article details President Donald Trump's involvement in commemorating the 4th of July amid weather-related delays. Trump delivered a speech at Mount Rushmore, a monument featuring four U.S. presid...

item.title: Paul McCartney performs 'I Want To Hold Your Hand' at Taylor Swift's wedding
item.description: Paul McCartney made a historic performance at Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding in New York's Madison Square Garden, playing 'I Want To Hold Your Hand' for the first time since 1964. The star-st...

item.title: Djokovic Breaks Federer's Record at Wimbledon, Advances to Quarter-Finals
item.description: Novak Djokovic etched his name into Wimbledon history by surpassing Roger Federer's singles match-win record, securing his 106th men's match victory at the grass-court Grand Slam. The Serbian star def...

item.title: Lagos Flooding Highlights Need for Safer Property Investments and Urban Resilience
item.description: The recent flooding in Lagos, which disrupted homes, businesses, and transportation across multiple areas, has reignited discussions about smarter property investment and urban resilience. Real estate...

item.title: Trump criticizes Democratic proposal to reform the Supreme Court and eliminate the Senate filibuster
item.description: President Donald Trump reacted strongly on social media after reports emerged that a group of progressive House Democrats had drafted a resolution proposing major institutional reforms. According to t...

item.title: Report Describes Frustration Among Trump Supporters After Delays and Disruptions at July 4 Fireworks Event

item.title: Marcos urges further study on proposal to lower minimum age of criminal responsibility amid youth crime concerns
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has said that the proposal to lower the age of criminal liability in the Philippines requires further study, especially in light of recent violent incidents involving mi...

item.title: Legal Dispute Over Procedural Delays in Tanzanian Opposition Leader's Treason Case
item.description: Tanzania's opposition leader, Tundu Lissu, has filed a compensation claim against the Director of Public Prosecutions (DPP) over delays in the Court of Appeal's review of his treason case. The legal b...

item.title: Pakistan's IT Minister Proposes Telecom Bill to Modernize 5G Infrastructure
item.description: Information Technology Minister Shaza Fatima Khawaja emphasized the urgency of the proposed telecom bill to address the inadequacies of existing laws in supporting modern technologies like 5G. The bil...

item.title: Albanese begins Pacific diplomacy tour with Fiji security pact and regional talks
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has begun a three-day diplomatic visit across the Pacific, starting in Fiji where he is expected to sign a new security agreement known as the Vuvale Union with Fijian ...

item.title: Albanese begins Pacific diplomatic tour with Fiji security pact and regional talks
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has arrived in Fiji to begin a three-day diplomatic visit across the Pacific aimed at strengthening Australia's relationships with key regional partners and reinforcing...

item.title: Tanzania government promotes digital reforms and youth inclusion in cooperatives
item.description: The Tanzanian government has called for cooperative societies to modernize operations and expand inclusivity, emphasizing their role in economic development and social stability. During the 2026 Inter...

item.title: High Court Acquits Teacher and Carpenter in Kigoma Murder Case Due to Insufficient Evidence
item.description: In a recent judgment delivered on Friday, July 3, 2026, at the High Court Kigoma Sub-Registry, three men from Tanzania were acquitted of murder charges. The accused, Mr Barnabas Andrea who works as a ...

item.title: AI Efficiency Drive and Its Effects on Global Jobs: Why Some Inefficiency May Still Be Needed
item.description: This article by Tope Fasua wey dey discuss how the world dey pursue efficiency too much through Artificial Intelligence. The writer talk say maybe the problem no be just efficiency, but we need to loo...

item.title: Leclerc leads the British Grand Prix as Hamilton battles Verstappen for a podium place at Silverstone
item.description: The live coverage follows the 2026 Formula 1 British Grand Prix at Silverstone, where Ferrari's Charles Leclerc leads the race during the closing stages after an intense strategic battle with Mercedes...

item.title: Britain says Russian hybrid operations have intensified amid continued support for Ukraine
item.description: Britain is facing what security experts describe as an increasingly aggressive Russian hybrid warfare campaign linked to London's military and political support for Ukraine. According to the report, r...

item.title: Cheslin Kolbe highlights areas for improvement after Springboks' win over England
item.description: Cheslin Kolbe, the Springboks' try-scoring winger, acknowledged that his team is 'far from perfect' following their 45-21 victory over England in the Nations Championship opener. Despite the win, Kolb...
